module_mood_contract: Unsettled competence. 'I wonder if this is right?' Multi-agent works; the output sits at an uneasy distance from trust. Doubt is planted, deliberately unresolved. Any edit that makes the output feel clean steals Module 5's teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'When to split an agent (and how)' and 'Debugging stuck agents' are both placed AFTER the retrieval and synthesis exercises. 'Agent that takes action' (demo) is an opener showing a ceiling — correctly placed before the exercises. Placement honours the opener/closer distinction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-exercise content is: one boundary-shift paragraph + one demo lecture link. No framework mapping, no practitioner-term naming, no tradeoff explanation before the exercises start. 'When to split' stays as closer. Dosage is minimal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 (Three retrievers): three agent sessions run concurrently while the student watches. Phase 2 (Three minds): subagents run in parallel inside one session. Both are walk-away-adjacent — the student sets up and observes, not reads-and-pastes every reply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: 50% magic rule (M3–M8).
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 is identified in content-strategy as one of the three magic beats (three-retrievers multi-session coordination). Four windows churning simultaneously is the magic moment. The magic is understandable: the student names seams in the Debrief.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Homework optional memory pass is marked with 'Skip if memory's still serving you.' No yoga-instructor pacing or forced pauses in the module spine. The closing 'Build' invitation is open, not commanded.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt contains the forcing functions: 'Audit harshly. Diplomatic summaries are a failing grade here.' / 'Name at least TWO handoff seams where the rules wobbled, with specific evidence: name the file, the pass, and what was lost.' The body callout AFTER the prompt explains why — it is post-run reflection, not a forcing function in prose. Speed-runner reads prompt, gets the force; the callout is a bonus explanation. Homework prompt: 'Name at least one drop candidate; an all-green health check means you didn't look hard enough.' — in the prompt, not body. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Measure silent failure, not just friction.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Content-strategy explicitly states 'The output is meant to feel 7/10, not 10/10 — that unease is the handoff to Module 5.' The module body says 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays.' Frictionless = wrong edit is baked into the design. No silent-failure risk: the module is designed so that competent execution still lands the unease.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report and self-audit charity — verify at the artifact, not the summary.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt: 'Name at least TWO handoff seams where the rules wobbled, with specific evidence: name the file, the pass, and what was lost. A seam without evidence doesn't count.' Homework prompt: 'Name at least one drop candidate; an all-green health check means you didn't look hard enough.' Both force artifact-level evidence over summary claims. Body callout reinforces: 'Diplomatic is the failure mode at a handoff seam.' PASS — behavior eval concurs (risks flagged as med-confidence, mitigated).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both fenced prompt blocks (Debrief + homework) contain no [BRACKET] placeholders. Debrief prompt references agents/, sources/, module-3/ as deterministic paths. Homework prompt references memory/ and sources/ as deterministic paths.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt has lead-in (implicit in the 'Debrief' section heading + five-minute context sentence). Homework prompt has 'run this between modules to let the new evidence pressure-test the memory's shape' as lead-in before the block. Both satisfy the semantic lead-in requirement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompt blocks use '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' label with blank line before fenced block. No blockquote wrapping, no '*(end of prompt)*' closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt block uses no **bold** or *italic* markdown inside the fence. Homework prompt block uses no markdown shout inside the fence. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt references 'agents/, sources/, module-3/' and 'CLAUDE.md at the root' — root reference uses positional description, not naked filename, and the intent (root-level file) is clear. Homework prompt references 'memory/ and sources/' as explicit subpaths. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 mood contract per content-strategy: 'Unsettled competence — I wonder if this is right?' The module body honours this explicitly: 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays. Hold it as a question, not a fix.' Key Concepts name seams and beige-averaging as where failure lives — all mood-aligned. Big Idea ('Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.') names the mechanism without resolving the quality question.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood not punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module's closing unease is 'I wonder if this is right?' — not-knowing, not having-done-wrong. The Debrief forces harsh audit of the SYSTEM (agents, seams, handoff rules), never of the student. The bridge to M4 is 'what's the worst thing it could do?' — framing the system, not the student. No shame framing detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.' — positive discipline (orchestration, coordination). 'Seams are where it fails' appears in Key Concepts as the negative case the discipline addresses, not the title framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Content-strategy M3 section: two shapes (separate sessions + subagents), Rumelt synthesizer spine, three stances (planner/experimenter/reframer), output 7/10 by design, unease as handoff to M5. Module file matches all: two shapes named in Key Concepts, Rumelt named as synthesizer spine, three stances named, mood unresolved at close. No front-running of M4/M5 teaching moments.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bootstrap themes from content-strategy: compounding, multi-agent orchestration, seam discipline, framework-guided judgment. Key Concepts bullets: 'Two multi-agent shapes' (orchestration theme), 'Splitting earns its keep when agents genuinely can't be one' (compounding/discipline theme), 'Seams are where it fails' (seam discipline theme), 'Three stances beat one summarizer' (orchestration theme), 'Frameworks are the synthesizer's spine' (judgment theme). At least 3 theme instances present. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets are tactical in the module's own voice. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Breadcrumb-style bolding is present naturally in the bullets (e.g., 'Splitting earns its keep when...'). Engineer register throughout, no strategy-doc vocabulary leaking into student-facing bullets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — callout stays mood-aligned, doesn't collapse unease into resolution.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The 'audit harshly' callout in the Debrief body is explicitly a reflection on WHY the harder ask works — it does not resolve the strategic unease ('I wonder if this is right?'). The callout explains audit mechanics at the seam level, not the strategic-output level. The closing paragraph explicitly preserves the unease: 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays.' The harsh-audit framing is mood-aligned with unsettled competence: harsh audit surfaces more to be uncertain about, not less. Risk (per eval prompt): callout might collapse unease into 'we found the problems so we fixed them.' Verdict: it does not — the callout stays at the agent/seam level, the strategic briefing's correctness is explicitly left open. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — seams as the site of failure.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment per content-strategy: 'Seams are where it fails. Synthesizer left un-prompted averages three takes into beige. Laziness is designed in.' Key Concepts bullet: 'Seams are where it fails. Conflicts get dropped, dialects clash, the synthesizer averages everything into beige. Where the pieces meet is where the lesson lives.' Debrief prompt forces seam-evidence: 'Name at least TWO handoff seams where the rules wobbled, with specific evidence.' Teaching moment is both named in Key Concepts AND structurally enforced in the Debrief prompt. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.' Exercise structure: Three retrievers (search), three synthesizer subagents + a synthesizer (decide). Filesystem is the literal shared medium. Big Idea matches the exercise design precisely. Retriever count and synthesizer count both map cleanly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — hands off to M4.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge to M4: 'You just built something that works across three systems. Which means it just gained access to three systems. What's the worst thing it could do with that?' — directly plants the M4 security/epistemic-humility mood. Content-strategy M4 connection: 'I'd like to be safe and I genuinely don't know how to know.' The bridge provokes the question without answering it. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Phase boundaries at pause beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module spine has natural pause points: after Name your crux (artifact: crux.md), after Three retrievers (artifact: sources/ files + memory/ sharpened), after Three minds (artifact: briefing), after lectures (mental model), after Debrief (CLAUDE.md rewritten). Each phase produces a tangible artifact or state change that marks the boundary. No run-on phases detected.

todos: Vocabulary-before-context: 'subagents' appears in What You'll Learn LO before the lecture that defines it. Not blocking (Teacher Claude bridges it in self-study; trainer in cohort), but polish candidate.

Body callout after Debrief prompt assumes student reads ahead before pasting. For self-study, Teacher Claude covers this; consider adding one sentence before the prompt block: 'Read the paragraph below the prompt after Claude responds.' — low-priority, Teacher Claude already compensates.
